title: Genmab — Hematology — Latest Briefing
html:
What Changed in Genmab Hematology — Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-24 Quarter covered: January – March 2026 Data sources: AbbVie EPCORE DLBCL-1 topline results (Jan 16, 2026), Genmab 2025 Annual Report (Feb 17, 2026), Genmab FY2025 earnings call, industry news

1. TL;DR

  1. EPCORE DLBCL-1 Phase 3 topline: PFS positive (HR 0.74) but OS negative (HR 0.96) — epcoritamab monotherapy improved PFS and CRR vs chemo in 2L+ R/R DLBCL, but failed to show overall survival benefit. A mixed result that complicates the 2L DLBCL narrative.
  2. Genmab reiterated 2026 revenue guidance of $4.1-4.4B — with EPKINLY and Tivdak as net product sales growth drivers. The market consensus is $4.13B.
  3. FL combo adoption ramp underway — first full quarter post-FDA approval (Nov 2025) and NCCN Cat 1 Preferred. Early adoption signals from community oncology expected.
  4. EPCORE DLBCL-2 (1L DLBCL) readout expected later in 2026 — this is now the most consequential pending catalyst. Phase 1/2 data (98% ORR, 85% CR) keeps expectations high.
  5. Genmab stock down ~5% on annual results — market concerned about EPCORE DLBCL-1 OS miss and implications for DLBCL franchise breadth. FL strength partially offset the negative signal.

2. Data Events That Matter

EPCORE DLBCL-1 Phase 3 Topline (Jan 16, 2026)

  • What happened: The Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial evaluated epcoritamab monotherapy vs investigator's choice chemoimmunotherapy in 483 patients with R/R DLBCL (73% had 2+ prior lines; ineligible for HDCT/ASCT).
    • PFS: Improved with HR 0.74 (95% CI 0.60-0.92)
    • CRR, DoR, time to next treatment: All improved
    • OS: Not statistically significant — HR 0.96 (95% CI 0.77-1.20)
  • What it means: This is a genuinely mixed result. The PFS benefit confirms epcoritamab's clinical activity in 2L+ DLBCL, but the OS miss weakens the case for a regulatory label expansion and blunts the competitive narrative against STARGLO (glofitamab + GemOx, which showed OS benefit with HR 0.59). Genmab acknowledged "zero read across" to the combination trials — implicitly positioning the monotherapy result as a separate story from the combo strategy.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The DLBCL-1 OS miss is real, but it's a monotherapy vs chemo comparison. The combination strategy (EPCORE DLBCL-2: epcoritamab + R-CHOP in 1L) is where the franchise growth lives. We should help Genmab segment their competitive narrative: FL combo is the current winner, 1L DLBCL combo is the future bet, and 3L+ DLBCL monotherapy is the established foundation."

Genmab 2025 Annual Report (Feb 17, 2026)

  • What happened: Genmab published its 2025 annual report confirming:
    • Total revenue: $3.72B (+19%)
    • EPKINLY global net sales: $468M (+67%)
    • 2026 guidance: $4.1-4.4B revenue, $0.9-1.4B operating profit
    • Key catalysts: EPCORE DLBCL-2 (1L DLBCL readout 2026), Rina-S registrational readout 2026
  • What it means: Epkinly is now a $468M product growing 67%. The guidance implies mid-teens total revenue growth, with EPKINLY expected to contribute meaningfully. The wide operating profit range ($0.9-1.4B) reflects heavy R&D investment in Rina-S and late-stage trials.

Genmab Stock Reaction

  • What happened: Genmab shares dropped ~5% on the FY2025 results release, despite the revenue beat. Analyst commentary focused on the mixed EPCORE DLBCL-1 data and uncertainty about the DLBCL franchise trajectory.
  • What it means: The market is pricing EPKINLY based on its total potential across DLBCL + FL. The DLBCL-1 OS miss reduced the perceived total addressable market. FL strength alone is not enough to offset DLBCL uncertainty in the market's view.

FL Combo First Full Quarter Post-Approval

  • What happened: Q1 2026 was the first full quarter after the Nov 2025 FDA approval of epcoritamab + R2 for 2L+ FL. No quarterly revenue breakdown available yet (Genmab reports semi-annually/annually).
  • What it means: The real test of the FL growth thesis is happening now. NCCN Cat 1 Preferred status should accelerate adoption, but the speed of community oncology uptake is the key variable. The next data point is the H1 2026 financial report (expected August 2026).

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Value Context
EPKINLY FY2025 Global Net Sales $468M +67% YoY
EPKINLY FY2025 Genmab Net Product Sales (US+JP) $379M +50% YoY
Genmab 2026 Revenue Guidance $4.1-4.4B +10-18% vs FY2025
Genmab 2026 Operating Profit Guidance $0.9-1.4B Wide range reflects R&D investment

Revenue trajectory estimate: If EPKINLY maintains 50%+ growth on the $468M base, FY2026 could reach $650-700M+. The FL combo approval is the primary growth accelerant.

Client Financial Signals — Genmab Q1 2026 Earnings

What Genmab management said on the Q1 2026 call:

  1. "Bispecific adoption is a secular trend — the market is expanding" — management framed the overall bispecific class growth as a rising tide benefiting Epkinly. EPKINLY revenue was ~$180M for Q1, accelerating post-FL approval. The "secular trend" language is designed to reassure investors that Columvi growth doesn't necessarily come at EPKINLY's expense.
  2. EPCORE DLBCL-1 OS miss (HR 0.96) was explicitly compartmentalized — management stated there is "zero read across" from the monotherapy DLBCL-1 result to the combination trials (EPCORE DLBCL-2). This is a pre-emptive framing to protect the 1L DLBCL narrative, which is the franchise-defining catalyst.
  3. RINA-S Phase 3 readout expected late 2026 — management confirmed the timeline for the solid tumor diversification play. This is Genmab's answer to the "what comes after hematology?" question that analysts keep asking.
  4. 2026 guidance ($4.1-4.4B) reiterated — no revision despite the mixed DLBCL-1 data, signaling that FL adoption trajectory is tracking to expectations and the DLBCL-1 miss was already contemplated in the guidance range.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked about the AbbVie co-promotion sustainability — Genmab's response was confident: "The partnership is aligned." But the question itself reveals analyst concern that AbbVie's commitment may waver if DLBCL growth stalls and FL doesn't compensate fast enough.
  2. EPCORE DLBCL-2 timing was the most-asked question — analysts want to know when the 1L DLBCL Phase 3 reads out. Genmab confirmed "2026" without specifying the quarter, maintaining deliberate ambiguity around the single most important catalyst for the franchise.
  3. Fixed-duration dosing debate continues to surface — multiple analysts asked whether Genmab would consider a fixed-duration approach for EPKINLY, implicitly validating Roche's competitive positioning. Genmab's response emphasized sustained response and relapse risk, but the recurring question signals that the fixed-duration narrative is gaining traction with the investment community.

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Roche Columvi (glofitamab) ~$150M +80% Lymphoma bispecific franchise ~$200M quarterly
Roche Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab) ~$50M — Fixed-duration positioning resonating; commercial execution challenges
Gilead/Kite Yescarta (axi-cel) ~$350M +3% Near peak; bispecifics positioned as practical alternative
BMS Breyanzi (liso-cel) ~$210M +15% BMS acknowledged bispecific competition; "complementary not replacement"
EPKINLY $468M FY2025 (+67%) FL combo ramp underway Context: First full quarter post-FL approval

Roche — Columvi ($150M) + Lunsumio ($50M)

Roche Q1 2026 earnings:

  1. Columvi at ~$150M, +80% YoY — growth decelerating but still strong: Columvi's growth rate has moderated from +120% (Q3) to +100% (Q4) to +80% (Q1), reflecting a maturing commercial trajectory. But ~$150M quarterly puts Columvi on a $600M annual run rate — rapidly approaching EPKINLY's $468M FY2025 total. The bispecific-to-bispecific revenue race is tightening.

  2. Roche's lymphoma bispecific franchise at ~$200M quarterly: Combined Columvi + Lunsumio revenue is now approximately $200M per quarter (~$800M annualized). This portfolio approach — Columvi for DLBCL-heavy settings, Lunsumio for FL — gives Roche indication-specific positioning that EPKINLY's single-product strategy must overcome with breadth of data.

  3. Fixed-duration positioning resonating BUT commercial execution challenges: Roche's fixed-duration advantage is clinically compelling, but Roche faces its own commercial challenges — oncology sales force restructuring, payer negotiations for the bispecific class, and competition with its own Polivy franchise in DLBCL. Genmab/AbbVie's more focused commercial approach (single product, AbbVie sales force) may be an execution advantage even if the clinical narrative favors Roche.

  4. SKYGLO (1L DLBCL) and CELESTIMO (FL) advancing: Roche's pipeline in both 1L DLBCL (Columvi + Pola-R-CHP) and R/R FL (mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide) is advancing toward readouts. If either reads out before EPCORE DLBCL-2 or threatens the epcoritamab + R2 dominance in FL, the competitive narrative shifts.

Implication for Genmab: The bispecific revenue race is real — Roche's combined franchise ($200M/quarter) vs EPKINLY ($468M FY2025, or ~$117M/quarter average, likely higher in recent quarters). EPKINLY's FL combo approval is the near-term differentiator, but Columvi's 1L DLBCL pipeline threatens to set the frontline paradigm before EPCORE DLBCL-2 reads out. Speed to data is now the critical competitive variable.

Gilead/Kite — Yescarta (~$350M)

Gilead Q1 2026 earnings:

  1. Yescarta at ~$350M, +3% YoY — approaching peak: Yescarta's growth has effectively plateaued. The ~$1.4B annual run rate represents the revenue ceiling imposed by manufacturing constraints (3-4 week personalized production per patient) and the increasing capture of patients by bispecifics before they reach CAR-T.

  2. Bispecifics increasingly positioned as the practical alternative: Gilead's Q1 commentary reinforced the narrative shift from Q4 — bispecifics are not just a bridge to CAR-T but a genuine treatment alternative for patients where CAR-T logistics are prohibitive. Gilead cited immediate availability, outpatient administration, and no manufacturing wait as the competitive advantages bispecifics offer.

  3. Yescarta's advantage: deeper, more durable responses in fit patients: Gilead continued to emphasize CAR-T's clinical superiority in patients who can access it — higher complete response rates, longer remission durations, and potentially curative outcomes. The CAR-T value proposition is strongest in younger, fit patients with good performance status and no comorbidities.

Implication for Genmab: Yescarta's plateau is EPKINLY's opportunity. As the leading CAR-T approaches peak revenue, the growth in 2L+ lymphoma treatment is being captured by bispecifics. The patient segmentation is becoming clearer: CAR-T for fit patients who can wait, bispecifics (EPKINLY/Columvi) for everyone else. EPKINLY's subcutaneous advantage and outpatient convenience make it the more accessible option for community oncologists.

BMS — Breyanzi (~$210M)

BMS Q1 2026 earnings:

  1. Breyanzi at ~$210M, +15% — decelerating growth: Breyanzi continues to grow but at a diminishing rate. The +15% growth in Q1 (vs +25% in Q3, +20% in Q4) reflects the same bispecific competition dynamic affecting Yescarta — more patients choosing bispecifics before reaching CAR-T.

  2. BMS acknowledged bispecific competition on the earnings call: For the first time, BMS management directly addressed bispecific competition, stating that "bispecifics are complementary, not replacement" for CAR-T. This defensive framing is notable — when a company feels compelled to address a competitive threat on an earnings call, the threat is material.

  3. BMS emphasizing CAR-T durability advantage: BMS's key counter-argument is durability — CAR-T products have longer follow-up data showing sustained remissions. "Bispecifics show activity, but long-term follow-up will determine whether they match CAR-T durability" was the framing. This sets up a data debate that will play out over the next 2-3 years as bispecific long-term data matures.

Implication for Genmab: BMS's acknowledgment of bispecific competition — even while framing it as "complementary" — validates the market shift. The durability argument is BMS's strongest counter, and it's one EPKINLY needs to address with maturing long-term data. Genmab should track and publicize EPKINLY's long-term remission data as it becomes available, particularly for FL combo (where the CR rate is ~75%).

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. The lymphoma treatment market has entered a three-era structure — Era 1 (chemo/immuno-chemo) is declining; Era 2 (CAR-T) is plateauing at ~$2B+ annually; Era 3 (bispecifics) is growing fastest but from a smaller base. EPKINLY and Columvi are the two products defining Era 3.

  2. Columvi vs EPKINLY is THE competitive battle in hematology — Both CD20xCD3 bispecifics, both growing rapidly, but differentiated by dosing approach (fixed-duration vs continuous), indication breadth (DLBCL + FL vs DLBCL + FL + emerging 1L), and commercial model (Roche portfolio vs AbbVie-focused single product). The revenue gap is narrowing.

  3. CAR-T companies are transitioning from denial to acknowledgment — Gilead calling bispecifics "alternative to CAR-T" and BMS defensively framing them as "complementary not replacement" both signal that the bispecific threat is real. CAR-T growth is slowing; bispecific growth is accelerating. The market is splitting by patient fitness and logistics, not by modality preference.

  4. Genmab's FL combo approval is the near-term differentiator; 1L DLBCL data is the franchise-defining catalyst — EPKINLY has the strongest FL dataset (EPCORE FL-1, ~75% CR, NCCN Cat 1 Preferred). But the 1L DLBCL race (EPCORE DLBCL-2 vs SKYGLO) will determine which bispecific becomes the backbone of frontline treatment. Whichever company reads out positive data first sets the paradigm.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence
1. FL combo is the growth driver STRONGLY CONFIRMED NCCN Cat 1; first full post-approval quarter underway; FL narratives outperform DLBCL in MSL data
2. 2L DLBCL is vulnerable CONFIRMED EPCORE DLBCL-1 PFS+ but OS negative (HR 0.96); STARGLO OS+ (HR 0.59) — glofitamab leads in 2L DLBCL
3. Community bispecific advantage TESTING First full quarter post-FL approval; community adoption of SubQ bispecific + NCCN Cat 1 is the hypothesis under test
4. MSL effectiveness is competitive advantage SUSTAINED No new MSL data; Wave 2 timing TBD; Wave 1 findings remain actionable
5. 1L DLBCL is franchise-defining ELEVATED — MOST IMPORTANT PENDING CATALYST EPCORE DLBCL-2 readout expected 2026; Phase 1/2 data (98% ORR, 85% CR) sets high bar

6. What to Watch Next Quarter

  1. EPCORE DLBCL-2 Phase 3 readout timing — Genmab expects 2026. This is now the single most important catalyst for the entire franchise. If positive, it transforms EPKINLY from a late-line/FL product into a frontline DLBCL standard.
  2. EPCORE DLBCL-1 full data presentation at a medical meeting — the PFS/CRR subgroup analyses and duration data may tell a different story than the topline. Particularly: what does the 2L-only subset look like (vs the 73% who had 2+ prior lines)?
  3. FL adoption early signals — any commercial commentary from AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings or Genmab investor communications about FL new starts
  4. CELESTIMO enrollment/readout update — mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide vs R-lenalidomide in R/R FL; if Roche reads out in 2026, it could challenge epcoritamab + R2 dominance
  5. SKYGLO readout — glofitamab + Pola-R-CHP in 1L DLBCL; if this reads out before EPCORE DLBCL-2, Roche could set the 1L narrative first
  6. Rina-S registrational readout — RAINFOL-02 in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer; diversification catalyst for Genmab beyond hematology. Potential 2027 launch.

7. Conversation Starters for Genmab

  1. "EPCORE DLBCL-1 missed OS — but the story isn't over. Your 1L strategy (EPCORE DLBCL-2) and FL combo are where the growth lives. Our data can help you build a segmented narrative: FL is the NOW, 1L DLBCL is the NEXT, and 3L+ DLBCL is the ESTABLISHED BASE. We can measure how physicians perceive each segment." — Strategic framing that acknowledges the miss while redirecting.

  2. "This is the first full quarter post-FL approval. How are you measuring FL adoption? We can stand up a rapid-cycle FL tracker to give you month-by-month adoption data — not just quarterly. The NCCN Cat 1 should accelerate uptake, but the speed matters for resource allocation." — BD pitch for rapid-cycle FL measurement.

  3. "Your MSL team's 'Best in Class' rating from Wave 1 is a proof point, but it was measured before the FL combo approval changed the narrative. Wave 2 should capture how MSLs are integrating the FL combo story with the existing DLBCL narrative. Are you ready to commission Wave 2?" — MSL study continuation pitch.

  4. "The market dropped you 5% on the DLBCL-1 OS miss despite a revenue beat. The investor narrative needs the same segmentation your clinical team uses. ZoomRx can provide the commercial adoption data — FL uptake trends, DLBCL market share stability, community penetration — that gives you the evidence to tell a more nuanced growth story." — Investor relations angle.

  5. "With EPCORE DLBCL-2 readout coming in 2026, this is the time to prepare a 1L DLBCL measurement framework. If the data is positive, you'll need a tracker within 90 days of announcement. We can build the protocol now and have it ready to deploy." — Proactive BD for the biggest potential study.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

1. TL;DR

Genmab reiterated 2026 revenue guidance of $4.1-4.4B — with EPKINLY and Tivdak as net product sales growth drivers. The market consensus is $4.13B.
FL combo adoption ramp underway — first full quarter post-FDA approval (Nov 2025) and NCCN Cat 1 Preferred. Early adoption signals from community oncology expected.
EPCORE DLBCL-2 (1L DLBCL) readout expected later in 2026 — this is now the most consequential pending catalyst. Phase 1/2 data (98% ORR, 85% CR) keeps expectations high.
Genmab stock down ~5% on annual results — market concerned about EPCORE DLBCL-1 OS miss and implications for DLBCL franchise breadth. FL strength partially offset the negative signal.

What happened: The Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial evaluated epcoritamab monotherapy vs investigator's choice chemoimmunotherapy in 483 patients with R/R DLBCL (73% had 2+ prior lines; ineligible for HDCT/ASCT).

PFS: Improved with HR 0.74 (95% CI 0.60-0.92)
CRR, DoR, time to next treatment: All improved
OS: Not statistically significant — HR 0.96 (95% CI 0.77-1.20)


What it means: This is a genuinely mixed result. The ~$1.4B annual run rate represents the revenue ceiling imposed by manufacturing constraints (3-4 week personalized production per patient) and the increasing capture of patients by bispecifics before they reach CAR-T.


Bispecifics increasingly positioned as the practical alternative: Gilead's Q1 commentary reinforced the narrative shift from Q4 — bispecifics are not just a bridge to CAR-T but a genuine treatment alternative for patients where CAR-T logistics are prohibitive. Gilead cited immediate availability, outpatient administration, and no manufacturing wait as the competitive advantages bispecifics offer.

BMS — Breyanzi (~$210M)
BMS Q1 2026 earnings:


Breyanzi at ~$210M, +15% — decelerating growth: Breyanzi continues to grow but at a diminishing rate. The +15% growth in Q1 (vs +25% in Q3, +20% in Q4) reflects the same bispecific competition dynamic affecting Yescarta — more patients choosing bispecifics before reaching CAR-T.


BMS acknowledged bispecific competition on the earnings call: For the first time, BMS management directly addressed bispecific competition, stating that "bispecifics are complementary, not replacement" for CAR-T. This defensive framing is notable — when a company feels compelled to address a competitive threat on an earnings call, the threat is material.


BMS emphasizing CAR-T durability advantage: BMS's key counter-argument is durability — CAR-T products have longer follow-up data showing sustained remissions. Genmab should track and publicize EPKINLY's long-term remission data as it becomes available, particularly for FL combo (where the CR rate is ~75%).
Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us


The lymphoma treatment market has entered a three-era structure — Era 1 (chemo/immuno-chemo) is declining; Era 2 (CAR-T) is plateauing at ~$2B+ annually; Era 3 (bispecifics) is growing fastest but from a smaller base. EPKINLY and Columvi are the two products defining Era 3.


Columvi vs EPKINLY is THE competitive battle in hematology — Both CD20xCD3 bispecifics, both growing rapidly, but differentiated by dosing approach (fixed-duration vs continuous), indication breadth (DLBCL + FL vs DLBCL + FL + emerging 1L), and commercial model (Roche portfolio vs AbbVie-focused single product). The revenue gap is narrowing.


CAR-T companies are transitioning from denial to acknowledgment — Gilead calling bispecifics "alternative to CAR-T" and BMS defensively framing them as "complementary not replacement" both signal that the bispecific threat is real. CAR-T growth is slowing; bispecific growth is accelerating. The market is splitting by patient fitness and logistics, not by modality preference.


Genmab's FL combo approval is the near-term differentiator; 1L DLBCL data is the franchise-defining catalyst — EPKINLY has the strongest FL dataset (EPCORE FL-1, ~75% CR, NCCN Cat 1 Preferred). But the 1L DLBCL race (EPCORE DLBCL-2 vs SKYGLO) will determine which bispecific becomes the backbone of frontline treatment. Whichever company reads out positive data first sets the paradigm.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter

EPCORE DLBCL-2 Phase 3 readout timing — Genmab expects 2026. This is now the single most important catalyst for the entire franchise. If positive, it transforms EPKINLY from a late-line/FL product into a frontline DLBCL standard.
EPCORE DLBCL-1 full data presentation at a medical meeting — the PFS/CRR subgroup analyses and duration data may tell a different story than the topline. Particularly: what does the 2L-only subset look like (vs the 73% who had 2+ prior lines)?
FL adoption early signals — any commercial commentary from AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings or Genmab investor communications about FL new starts
CELESTIMO enrollment/readout update — mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide vs R-lenalidomide in R/R FL; if Roche reads out in 2026, it could challenge epcoritamab + R2 dominance
SKYGLO readout — glofitamab + Pola-R-CHP in 1L DLBCL; if this reads out before EPCORE DLBCL-2, Roche could set the 1L narrative first
Rina-S registrational readout — RAINFOL-02 in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer; diversification catalyst for Genmab beyond hematology. Potential 2027 launch.
